markdown:
#  Why isn't the project's budget view reflecting labor costs and production quantities from timecard entries? 
##  Answer 
To track labor productivity in your project's budget, you can use the Real-Time or Procore Labor Productivity Cost budget view. To ensure your budget includes both existing and future timecard information, you must [configure two settings in the Company Timesheets tool](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/timesheets-company/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-company-level-timesheets):
  * Set the default cost type in the Timesheets tool to include data from future timecard entries.
  * Apply that default cost type in the Timesheets tool to include data from existing timecard entries.

  * [Which Procore tools can I use to add production quantities to my project's budget?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-procore-tools-can-i-use-to-add-production-quantities-to-my-projects-budget)
  * [What are Procore's standard budget views?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-standard-budget-views)
title: Why isn't the project's budget view reflecting labor costs and production quantities from timecard entries?
description: Describes important configuration settings in the Timesheets tool for companies and projects using the Real Time Labor Productivity and Procore Labor Productivity Cost budget views.
